county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clay County formed 1851 from Indian lands. County seat is Spencer.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Spencer holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Clay County was settled in the 1870s after the railroad reached Spencer. The county was part of the Spirit Lake region affected by the 1857 Spirit Lake Massacre (Inkpaduta's raid), which delayed settlement. Clay County Heritage Foundation and Spencer Public Library local history room. Scandinavian and German settlers dominate. Royal and Everly communities. Little Sioux River. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Clay County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Spencer
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Spencer
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Spencer / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Clay County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Clay County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Clay County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Clay County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Clay County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Clay County Auditor, Spencer
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Clay County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for Clay County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clayton County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Elkader.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Elkader holds deeds from 1840; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Clayton County along the Mississippi was among the earliest settled areas of Iowa. Elkader, named for Algerian leader Abd el-Kader, reflects founder Timothy Davis's interests. German and Luxembourger immigrants settled heavily in Clayton County -- Luxembourger Heritage Society records. Volga River and Turkey River valleys. Guttenberg riverfront community with strong German heritage. Clayton County Historical Society. Marquette and McGregor river towns. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Clayton County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1837. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1837. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clinton County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Clinton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Clinton holds deeds from 1841; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Clinton County is an important Mississippi River county. The city of Clinton was a major lumber milling center in the 1860s-1890s -- lumber company records document workers. Clinton County Historical Society holds extensive collections. German Catholic and Lutheran church records are strong. Lyons and DeWitt communities have separate histories. Clinton Herald newspaper archives. Clinton Community College archives. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Clinton County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Crawford County formed 1851 from Shelby. County seat is Denison.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Denison holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Crawford County was settled by German, Danish, and Swedish immigrants in the 1860s-1870s. Denison later became home to a significant Latino community in the meatpacking era. Crawford County Historical Society. Donna Reed Heritage Museum in Denison. Schleswig and Kiron German communities. Boyer River valley settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online. Danish Lutheran church records for the Charter Oak area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Crawford County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dallas County formed 1846 from Polk. County seat is Adel.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Adel holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Dallas County is adjacent to Des Moines and has been one of Iowa's fastest-growing counties. Early settlers came from Ohio, Indiana, and Pennsylvania. Raccoon River valley communities. Dallas County Historical Society in Adel. Perry was a major Chicago, Milwaukee & St. Paul Railroad town with significant Italian and Scandinavian worker communities. Van Meter and Waukee communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Dallas County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Davis County formed 1843 from Van Buren. County seat is Bloomfield.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bloomfield holds deeds from 1844; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Davis County in southeastern Iowa was settled by migrants from Virginia, Kentucky, and Tennessee in the 1840s. Strong southern cultural influence. Drakesville and Troy communities. Davis County Historical Society. Fox River and Soap Creek valley settlements. Camp meeting and Methodist church records are important. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch microfilm. The county's southern Iowa location means Appalachian migration patterns are relevant for tracing family origins.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Davis County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Decatur County formed 1846 from Appanoose. County seat is Leon.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Leon holds deeds from 1851; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Decatur County was a significant Mormon Trail corridor -- the Garden Grove settlement (1846) was a major LDS staging point. LDS Church History Library in Salt Lake City holds emigrant records. Non-Mormon settlers arrived from Missouri and the upper South. Decatur County Historical Museum in Leon. Nine Eagles State Park area. Grand River valley settlements. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch microfilm.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Decatur County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Delaware County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Manchester.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Manchester holds deeds from 1842; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Delaware County was settled by New Englanders and German/Luxembourgish immigrants. Manchester was a mill town on the Maquoketa River. Delaware County Historical Society in Hopkinton. Strong German Catholic parishes in Dyersville area (Basilica of St. Francis Xavier). Earlville and Colesburg communities. Sand Springs area early settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Des Moines County formed 1834 from Original county (Michigan Territory). County seat is Burlington.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Burlington holds deeds from 1834; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Des Moines County is one of Iowa's two original counties (1834, Michigan Territory). Burlington served as the first capital of Iowa Territory (1838-1841). Earliest Iowa records are here. Burlington Public Library has strong local history collections. Des Moines County Historical Society. German and Irish immigrant communities along the Mississippi. Catholic and Lutheran church records date from the 1830s. Crapo Park and Heritage Hill areas. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online. Burlington Hawk Eye newspaper archives from 1839.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Des Moines County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Burlington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1836. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Burlington / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1836. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Des Moines County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Des Moines County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Des Moines County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Des Moines County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Des Moines County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Des Moines County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Des Moines County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Des Moines County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Des Moines County Auditor, Burlington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Des Moines County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Des Moines County Early Church Records -- Mississippi River Settlements
source_profiles.repository: Various -- diocesan archives, denominational archives, FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Church registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) from early Mississippi River settlements in Des Moines County. Catholic, Lutheran, Methodist, and Presbyterian records. German and Irish congregations predominate.

source_profiles.source_name: Des Moines County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: State Historical Society of Iowa / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Des Moines County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, land sale notices. State Historical Society of Iowa holds microfilm of many Iowa newspapers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Dickinson County formed 1851 from Indian lands (Spirit Lake region). County seat is Spirit Lake.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Spirit Lake holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Dickinson County was the site of the 1857 Spirit Lake Massacre (Inkpaduta's Wahpekute band), which dramatically delayed settlement until the 1860s. The Iowa Great Lakes (Spirit Lake, West Okoboji, East Okoboji) attracted resort communities. Dickinson County Historical Society at Spirit Lake. Arnolds Park Amusement Park history. Scandinavian settlers arrived via railroad. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Dickinson County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Spirit Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Spirit Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Spirit Lake / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Dickinson County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Dickinson County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Dickinson County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Dickinson County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Dickinson County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Dickinson County Auditor, Spirit Lake
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Dickinson County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.
